title: Senecio berteroi
intro: Senecio berteroi, commonly known as Berter's ragwort, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region, to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Senecio berteroi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal remedies, such as cough syrups and expectorant tonics.
taxonomy: Senecio berteroi belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Senecio, commonly known as ragworts or old man's hay.
